name: vault.getEntity returns stripped normalized form
description: vault.getEntity() returns the normalized (stripped) form for canvases — no nested items, no resolved references. Extensions and nested structures only present in denormalized export. Callers needing full canvas data must use vault.export() or denormalize() explicitly.

Dual mutation paths (vault.update vs vault.dispatch) don't coordinate. Path A shallow-merges onto entity. Path B snapshots state, runs reducer, replaces NormalizedState wholesale via vault.restore(). No locking/queue/merge — mid-debounce metadata edits silently lost when dispatch overwrites with stale snapshot.
resolution: Unify mutation through single action-dispatch path, or add optimistic locking so restore detects and merges concurrent edits.

description: Two independent undo/redo systems: HistoryStore (denormalized trees, App.svelte) and ActionHistory (JSON patches, never wired to UI). Undo only covers load-based mutations. vault.update() metadata edits never pushed to history — Ctrl+Z does nothing or reverts to much earlier state. History comparison is O(n) JSON.stringify on megabyte trees.
resolution: Wire ActionHistory incremental JSON patches to UI undo, or replace HistoryStore with action-based undo capturing all mutation paths.

description: Extension data (IIIF extensions on entities) orphaned during vault.update(). Shallow-merge in updateEntity() replaces top-level keys but extensions nested under separate normalized path. Update drops extension data if payload doesn't include it.
resolution: Make updateEntity() extension-aware: deep-merge extension paths or require explicit extension updates through dedicated API.

content: $state.raw destructuring breaks reactivity. Vault uses $state.raw for performance on large normalized entity maps. Destructuring outside reactive context loses change tracking. Always access through store reactive getters.

description: XSS protection bypass: DOMPurify sanitization runs only during normalize() (import path) but NOT in vault.dispatch() annotation actions. actions/annotation.ts adds annotations via addEntity() without sanitization. Runtime-created annotations bypass XSS protection.
resolution: Move sanitization into addEntity() or validate annotation body in all action reducers. Should sanitize in both import and runtime creation paths.

description: Partial batch success semantics in trash operations. BATCH_RESTORE continues processing after individual entity failures, returns partial state change. Undo records the 'successful' batch but only some entities were restored. Undo/redo produces inconsistent state for partial operations.
resolution: Implement all-or-nothing transaction semantics for batch operations. Validate all entities before mutating, or roll back on any failure.

description: Missing validation at vault operation level. reorderChildren() does not validate IDs exist, are unique, or array is complete. moveEntity() does not validate newParentId exists. Validation only in action handlers, not operations — direct callers or new action paths bypass guards.
resolution: Push validation into operation functions (movement.ts, updates.ts) not just action handlers. Defense in depth.

name: Silent return pattern in vault operations
description: Multiple vault functions silently return original state on error: updateEntity if entity missing, trashEntity if type unknown, moveEntity if parent not found, removeEntity if not in index. No logging, no error context. Makes debugging silent no-ops extremely difficult.

content: Type casting proliferation in denormalization/normalization. Multiple 'as unknown as Record<string, unknown>' casts for annotation.body, painting detection, extension extraction. Root cause: IIIFAnnotation.body typed as generic unknown[]. Needs AnnotationBody union type.

description: Inconsistent type index repair across code paths. updateEntity() has fallback repair (linear scan entities if typeIndex stale), but trash.ts, queries.ts, and movement.ts silently return state when typeIndex lookup fails. Stale index corruption handled in one path but silently ignored in others.
resolution: Either repair typeIndex in all paths or add centralized integrity check. At minimum, log warnings on typeIndex misses.
